item.title: Wild
item.dc:creator: kixxarse1986

item.title: You were the best you could be
item.description: The young man here is our son Austin. He was taken from our lives Sunday afternoon in a tragic accident through no fault of his own. He was 32 and a damn fine son, brother, husband, and father. He was a surveyor by trade and a good person by choice. Any way to describe him falls short. There is now one less good person on the planet. He would give you the shirt off his back, shoes off his feet, or the last $2 in his pocket. All he wanted was to be a better man. Son, you were the best you could be and we are proud to tell people about you. Our new normal is without you and damn that's gonna be tough. You were everything to all of us and your absence leaves a massive void in our lives that only your memory can fill.
item.dc:creator: RagingSlab

item.title: Have a great Wednesday Imgur!
item.description:
I want one desperately.
Guilty.
Neil Gaiman and Sir Terry Pratchett tax because they bring me joy :) Edit: I think in #1 the beds are actually created that way for sustainability purposes as opposed to obstructing fun times, but it still gave me a giggle

item.title: Update to a 5 year old imgur comment
item.description:
5 years ago, my boyfriend commented on a post about Chrono Trigger.
@Bertthechimneysweep Congrats, you called it! We got married on February 29th this year.

item.dc:creator: supastebbilicious

item.title: movie scenes inspired by art
item.description:
Shutter Island - Klimt's Kiss
Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (George Lucas) - The Fallen Angel (Alexandre Cabanel)
Alien 3 (David Fincher) - Girl’s Head beside a Skull (Rex Whistler)
The Truman Show (Peter Weir) - Architecture au clair de lune (René Magritte)
The Dreamers (Bernardo Bertolucci) - Venus de Milo (attributed to Alexandros of Antioch, 2nd century BC) Eva Green—wearing black arm length gloves—stands in the dark, gracefully recreating one of the most iconic sculptures of all time.
Dunkirk (Christopher Nolan) - Wanderer above the Sea of Fog (Caspar David Friedrich
Lust for Life (Vincente Minnelli) - The Night Café (Vincent van Gogh)
About Schmidt (Alexander Payne) - The Death of Marat (Jacques-Louis David)
A Clockwork Orange (Stanley Kubrick) - Prisoners' Round (Vincent van Gogh)
The Return (Andrey Zvyagintsev) - Lamentation of Christ (Andrea Mantegna)
Marie Antoinette (Sofia Coppola) - Napoleon Crossing the Alps (Jacques-Louis David)
Metropolis (Fritz Lang) - The Tower of Babel (Pieter Bruegel the Elder)
Nighthawks (Edward Hopper) - Pennies from Heaven (Herbert Ross)
Dreams (Akira Kurosawa) - Wheatfield with Crows (Vincent van Gogh) Film legend Martin Scorsese plays the role of the Dutch painter in Kurosawa's 1990 movie.
Django Unchained (Quentin Tarantino) - The Blue Boy (Thomas Gainsborough)
The Exorcist (William Friedkin) - The Empire of Lights (René Magritte)
Pan's Labyrinth (Guillermo del Toro) - Saturn Devouring His Son (Francisco Goya)
Labyrinth (Jim Henson) - Relativity (M.C. Escher)
Inherent Vice (Paul Thomas Anderson) - The Last Supper (Leonardo da Vinci)
Shirley: Visions of Reality (Gustav Deutsch) - Morning Sun (Edward Hopper)
Melancholia (Lars Von Trier) - Ophelia (Sir John Everett Millais)
Malena (Giuseppe Tornatore) - Georgette Magritte (René Magritte)
The Adventures of Baron Munchausen (Terry Gilliam) - The Birth of Venus (Sandro Botticelli) When asked about the beauty of Botticelli's masterpiece, Gilliam replied, "You don’t do much better than that. I think he really cracked that one."
Shirley: Visions of Reality (Gustav Deutsch) - New York Movie (Edward Hooper)
Scream (Wes Craven) - The Scream (Edvard Munch) Director Wes Craven has expressed his admiration for Munch, describing the painting as "a classic reference to pure fear."
MASH and the Last Supper Sauce: https://x.com/JamesLucasIT/status/1795507471693320546
item.dc:creator: aycaramba

item.title: Images of Note: Feral girl summer
item.description:
Oh no
I mean...
Kinda wild how stories are so important.
I have to take meds because of high school!
Doesn't doing thing manually defeat the whole purpose of AI.
http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii Eeeeeeeeeh
http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii Sometimes you have to call the pope to find your weed.
Fuck WEBP.
Yeah fuck family vlogs
YEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEES
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay Also STREEEEEEEAM: http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii

item.title: Even the bear is like 'smh'.
item.description:
This is important.
TLDR below: word wall trigger warning! `It's not his fault you have a nice ass.` `It's not a big deal, this stuff happens all the time. ` 'You're overreacting.' Michele Howley posted this about what happened to her as she was walking out of a concert. It’s been shared over 200,000 times on her page. It will take you about 90 seconds to read it. And I especially like the last line. Here’s what she wrote: On Saturday night my mom, Grandma & I went to see a concert. After the encore, we filed into the stream of people leaving the event. That’s when I felt something hit me from behind, specifically hitting the right side of my butt. I turned around and a group of guys were walking behind me. They snickered. I thought, "It was probably an accident. people bump into each other all the time at concerts." I thought I heard someone say, "Do it again." But I dismissed it, thinking I probably misheard. Less than a minute later, I felt someone slowly run their fingers over my right butt cheek from the bottom & squeeze. I was wearing a long, velvet skirt - I could feel it all - and the heat of his hand. I whipped around, I pushed him backward and I screamed "Don't you ever f***ing touch a woman like that! ' He gave me a dopey face and put his hands up in the air, as if pleading innocence. Two security personnel who happened to be standing by grabbed him and motioned for me to follow. I was hot with anger. As we walked toward security, his friend ran up to match my pace. He said: “Come on, it's a compliment How can you blame him? It's not his fault you have a nice ass.” We walked together into the security office and immediately the conversation switched to: "My friend would never to do that. He didn't touch her. And a familiar chorus of "she's a liar." The police came and asked me if I'd like to press charges for battery. I never even considered I could. Stunned, I said I didn't know. They told me that I'd have to stay for a lot longer while they did the paperwork, that I'd have to show at not one, but probably multiple court dates. They said it would be hard to prove. My anger faltered and a small voice emerged in my head, "Is it really that big of a deal? He just grabbed my butt. Stuff like this does happen all the time. I told them I would think about it. It was their word against mine. I sat down at the table that my Mom, Grandma & Kevin, the head of security, were sitting at. He looked at me and said immediately, "You need to press charges, he shouldn't get away with it. I have a daughter." My mom and Grandma obviously agreed and that small voice faded as livid anger took the drivers seat once again. Why WASN'T it a big deal that man felt entitled to not only touch my butt once, but confidently and invasively grab it for a second time? Why was I expected to take it as a compliment? Why was I worried about burdening the police officers with paper work? Why was I worried about being believed? Why was my first reaction anger, quickly followed by self doubt? Why was it "just an ass grab"? As we waited, I thought to myself that maybe it had to start with not dismissing the small things. To stop saying, "it's just an ass grab." To stop dismissing what we've been taught as women to withstand and tolerate. The police came back after awhile and asked if I'd decided to press charges for battery. I gave my answer with the same confidence that he grabbed my ass with. A minute later, the man was handcuffed and led outside where the police car waited. He would have to post bail, hire an attorney and see me in court. I didn't care if I couldn't prove that it happened in front of a judge. I'd take it as far as it could go. In part for myself, yes, but really for all women (and men) who know this same experience far too well, and who experience far, far worse. I'm choosing to silence the small voice that dismisses experiences like this one - that makes us feel like an inconvenience for speaking up. I'm saying goodbye to the temptation to believe "it's just an ass grab." "You grabbed the wrong right butt cheek, homie. "I'll see you in court." TLDR Guy gropes a girl at a concert, *twice*, and is taught the error of his ways. Even the bear is like, dude, wtf?
item.dc:creator: Heavenissize17socks

item.title: New Species of Anaconda Measures a Record-Breaking 26 Feet - Freek Vonk, a wildlife biologist and TV presenter Swims Up to It .... Good Idea??
item.description: Not sure why the guy is dressed for a BBQ in the Hamptons. The body is as thick as a car tire. The animal measures about 26 feet and weighs around 440 pounds. The new species is found in northern South America and includes Venezuela, Suriname, and French Guyana. Although the animal looks identical to the green anaconda, its genetic makeup is 5.5% different. “To put this in perspective: humans and chimps are genetically different only about 2% from each other,” https://www.instagram.com/p/C5AhGLitiVc/

item.description: I'm not the conspiracy type, but it seems like they wanted to influence the general public so they think the economy is bad, just so a certain candidate would be voted into office to slash regulations and taxes on the 1%.
